name: Total Vulnerability Count (30-day trend)
natural_language_question: How many vulnerabilities do we have, and how is the total count changing over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily table which contains one row per vulnerability finding per asset per day
Filter to active vulnerability states and exclude scan metadata rows
Group by snapshot_date and count distinct (asset, cve_id) pairs to compute a daily vulnerability count
Restrict the date range to the last 30 days to produce the trend series
Optionally compute a 7-day moving average to smooth daily volatility
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Requires silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily to be built from the bronze Qualys ingest

name: Vulnerability Count by Severity (trend)
natural_language_question: How many vulnerabilities exist in each severity tier, and how has each tier trended over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which includes a normalized severity field
Filter to active findings and group by snapshot_date and severity_normalized
Count distinct (asset, cve_id) for each severity per day
Produce a time series for each severity for the last 30 days to visualize tiered trends
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Severity normalization required in silver table for consistent grouping

name: Critical Vulnerability Trend (30 days)
natural_language_question: How many CRITICAL vulnerabilities are present each day and is the count improving or worsening over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
Filter to severity_normalized = CRITICAL and exclude any findings marked as fixed/remediated
Group by snapshot_date and count distinct (asset, cve_id)
Produce a 30-day time series and calculate period-over-period change to highlight trend direction
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
sla_or_threshold: Alert if CRITICAL trend increases > 10% week-over-week
implementation_note: Requires consistent 'fixed' / 'remediated' state in bronze or silver to exclude remediated items

name: Unpatched Vulnerability Count (trend)
natural_language_question: How many vulnerabilities remain unpatched each day and how is that number changing over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily as the starting point
Filter to vulnerability findings where patch/fix state indicates not patched or patch available
Group by snapshot_date and count distinct (asset, cve_id) to compute daily unpatched counts
Create a 30-day series and compute rolling averages to visualize remediation velocity
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
sla_or_threshold: Target: reduce unpatched critical vuln count by 50% in 30 days
implementation_note: Patch state in Qualys is a proxy; integrating patch-management or ticketing systems improves accuracy

name: New Vulnerability Discovery Rate
natural_language_question: How many new vulnerabilities are discovered per day and how has discovery rate changed over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains discovery_date
For each snapshot_date count findings whose discovery_date equals snapshot_date to capture 'new' discoveries
Produce a daily time series over the last 30 days and compute a 7-day moving average
Highlight anomalies where daily new discoveries exceed baseline by a configurable threshold
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
implementation_note: Discovery date must be normalized in the silver table; anomalous spikes may indicate scanning scope changes

name: Median Age of Unremediated Vulnerabilities (trend)
natural_language_question: What is the median age (days since discovery) of unremediated vulnerabilities, and is the median decreasing over time?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains discovery_date and snapshot_date
Filter to active/unremediated vulnerabilities
Compute age in days as snapshot_date minus discovery_date for each record
Aggregate by snapshot_date to compute the median age per day and produce a 30-day trend
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
sla_or_threshold: Target median age <= 30 days for non-critical, <= 7 days for critical
implementation_note: Accurate discovery_date required; consider joining with ticketing system to confirm remediation dates

name: Vulnerability Count by Asset (trend)
natural_language_question: Which assets have the highest number of vulnerabilities and how have their counts changed over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains asset identifiers and snapshot_date
Filter to active findings and group by asset_id and snapshot_date counting distinct cve_id per asset
For each asset produce a daily time series and compute change versus the prior 30-day average
Rank assets by current vuln count and expose filters for severity and business criticality
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Asset identifiers must be normalized in silver table for reliable aggregation and joins

name: Top CVE Exposure (assets impacted over time)
natural_language_question: Which CVEs affect the most assets and how has each CVE's exposure changed over the last 30 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily with cve_id and asset identifiers
For each snapshot_date and cve_id count distinct assets affected
Rank CVEs by exposed asset count per day and select top N for trend visualization
Produce a time series per top CVE showing exposure changes across the selected period
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
implementation_note: Ranking and top-N selection are computed in gold or visualization layer from the silver time series

name: Scan Coverage (daily trend)
natural_language_question: What percentage of known assets are scanned by Qualys each day, and is coverage improving over time?
calculation_plan_steps: From the bronze qualys_vulnerabilities_api_2_0_fo_qid_search_list_dynamic_dynamic_list identify distinct assets observed per snapshot_date
Define expected asset population (either from an authoritative inventory or inferred baseline from the table over a 30-day window)
Compute daily coverage as (distinct assets scanned on snapshot_date) / (expected asset population) and produce a time series
Flag days where coverage falls below predefined thresholds for operational follow-up
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
implementation_note: Authoritative asset inventory integration recommended to make coverage calculation exact; current calculation may be an inferred baseline

name: Patch Compliance (Qualys proxy) Trend
natural_language_question: What is the patch compliance percentage (proxy) over time using Qualys fix/patch state fields, and is it improving?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the bronze qualys_vulnerabilities_api_2_0_fo_qid_search_list_dynamic_dynamic_list and silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily where fix/patch state is available
Classify each applicable vulnerability instance as 'patched' or 'non-patched' based on available state fields
For each snapshot_date compute patched / (patched + non-patched) to derive a daily patch compliance proxy percentage
Produce a 30-day trend and highlight periods of improvement or regression
mapped_control_codes: Vulnerabilities scanned and remediated
Vulnerability and system monitoring procedures established
sla_or_threshold: Target patch compliance proxy >= 90% for non-critical; higher expectation for critical
kpis_covered: Trend of patched vs unpatched findings
implementation_note: This is a proxy using Qualys fields; full patch compliance requires integration with patch-management systems (e.g., SCCM, WSUS, Jamf) and/or endpoint management
